head: a39d15e
rule: ContactMesh.Core stays provider-neutral; no Google/Microsoft/Graph/Workspace references.
updatePolicy: Update this file after every completed functional/docs/test slice before final response or commit.

bootstrap.instruction: New coding sessions should read AGENTS.md, then PROJECT_STATE.json, before continuing.

completed.note: Solution renamed/reorganized into src/tests/docs/samples/tools; OSS docs, gitignore, samples, provider shells.
Provider-neutral models/options/interfaces/sync planner/executor/merge shells.
Legacy GoogleUtils and Groups to Contacts migration sources preserved under ignored tools/migration paths.
Group visibility/member visibility and group mapping rules ported into Core; Google visibility mapper added.
Stale managed contacts delete only when no user-owned data remains; otherwise strip managed fields.
Managed duplicate contacts by primary email are merged and extras deleted.
Blank contacts and managed-email-only contacts can be pruned.
Included/excluded organization unit target eligibility rules, including =Ignore semantics.
Directory users map to managed MeshContact with work email, org fields, phones, labels, metadata.
Duplicate contact emails removed; provider-resolved send-as can become primary work email.
Policy-owned labels reconcile during merge while personal labels are preserved.
Visible groups can become managed contacts with group: source IDs.
All test projects use xunit.v3 3.2.2 and visualstudio runner 3.1.5; analyzer warning fixed.
Google provider now has tested contact-group label ownership metadata and create/update/delete reconciliation planning without live API calls.
Added AGENTS.md bootstrap prompt and PROJECT_STATE.json handoff ledger.
Installed local Codex skill at C:/Users/ZunairFayaz/.codex/skills/continue for /Continue-style task selection, verification, ledger update, and commit workflow.
GoogleContactBatchWriter reconciles desired contact labels through a Google contact-group label client port before contact writes.
Added a live People API HTTP client behind IGoogleContactGroupLabelClient, with delegated access-token seam and fake-handler tests for list/create/update/delete contact-group label calls.
Added delegated service-account access-token acquisition for Google People API calls, with fake token-source tests and explicit contacts scope docs/sample config.
Google People contacts now read through a live HTTP client, write create/update/delete through the batch writer, map planner metadata, and emit provider-neutral dry-run log entries.
Added provider-neutral run orchestration for directory users, group visibility/exclusions, group mappings, factories, planner execution, and wired CLI/Worker hosts to run it.
Expanded provider-neutral sync state contract for checkpoints and per-contact provider IDs/etags, with a JSON file-backed store and persistence tests.
Added provider-neutral sync run summary aggregation, structured dry-run operation log fields, shared CLI/Worker dry-run report output, and tests.
Added shared host configuration binding for ContactMesh, SyncRule, Google Workspace, and Microsoft365 options across CLI/Worker/Web, with binding tests.
Replaced the Web placeholder with a server-rendered settings overview for runtime, rules, and provider options, with renderer tests for binding display, encoding, and secret masking.
Added Microsoft Graph client-credentials token request normalization and HTTP token source through MicrosoftGraphClientFactory, with fake-source and fake-handler tests.
Added a paged Microsoft Graph users client, mapped Graph users to MeshUser, and wired Microsoft365 host directory reads with fake-handler/provider tests.
Added a live Graph contacts client/provider for Outlook contact list/create/update/delete, source-id extended properties, category label mapping, host wiring, and fake-handler/provider tests.

Added live Graph group and transitive membership reads, mapped group users/org contacts into provider-neutral groups/contacts, and wired Microsoft365 host group reads with fake-handler/provider tests.

Updated README to reflect current provider/host state, .NET 8 build and test commands, config flow, CLI/Worker/Web run steps, provider setup notes, pending Docker hosted worker mode, and local appsettings ignore coverage.

Ran a live Microsoft365 dry-run with app credentials: Graph auth/users/groups/contact reads succeeded for mailbox-enabled targets; 297 targets produced 81,437 planned creates with writes skipped, and 24 targets reported MailboxNotEnabledForRESTAPI without aborting the run.

Added provider-neutral TargetUsers scoping so Microsoft365 dry-runs can limit sync recipients while preserving eligible directory users as source contacts; documented and surfaced the setting in samples/config/UI tests.

Added detailed Web settings descriptions for runtime, sync rules, provider credentials/scopes, dry-run safety, TargetUsers live-test scoping, and credential masking; renderer coverage now checks the guidance.

pending.note: Sample compose exists; production Docker/hosted worker mode not implemented.

blocked.note: Manual Microsoft365 dry-run succeeded with tenant credentials; automated live-provider test strategy is still pending and must not commit secrets.
Remote origin is configured but user said push later.
